title: Advanced proteomics approaches to unravel protein homeostasis.
abstract: Quantitative proteomics methods are instrumental in measuring the interplay between protein synthesis and protein degradation in cells and tissues in different conditions and substantially contribute to the understanding of control mechanisms for protein homeostasis. Proteomics and chemoproteomics approaches enable the characterization of small molecule modifiers of protein degradation for therapeutic applications. Here, we review recent developments and applications of mass spectrometry-based (chemo-)proteomics methods for the study of cellular homeostasis.
